sections.paragraph:
ματαιότης, ητος, ἡ (s. prec. entry; Philod., Rhet. II p. 26, 6 Sudh. μ. ἀνθρώπων; Sext. Emp., Adv. Math. 1, 278; Pollux 6, 134; LXX; TestSol 8:2 D; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 141. Perh. also CIG IV, 8743, 6) state of being without use or value, emptiness, futility, purposelessness, transitoriness τῇ μ. ἡ κτίσις ὑπετάγη the creation was subjected to frustration Ro 8:20. Of the heathen περιπατεῖν ἐν μ. τοῦ νοός walk with their minds fixed on futile things Eph 4:17. φεύγειν ἀπὸ πάσης μ. flee from all idle speculations 4:10; cp. Pol 7:2 (καθαρεύειν ἀπὸ πάσης μ. νοημάτων καὶ λέξεων Orig., C. Cels. 5, 46, 5). ὑπέρογκα ματαιότητος φθέγγεσθαι utter highsounding but empty words 2 Pt 2:18 (cp. Ps 37:13). ἐπὶ ματαιότητι out of folly (Arrian, Ind. 36, 1 ἐπὶ τῆς ἀγγελίης τῇ ματαιότητι) ITr 8:2.—DELG s.v. μάτη. M-M. TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

Philod Philod , I b.c.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 621
